artifact_family: IBM 80-column Hollerith punched card (FORTRAN coding deck)
era: 80-column rectangular-hole format introduced 1928; this FORTRAN deck ~1960s-1970s
mechanism: Passive storage medium. 80 columns x 12 rows = 960 hole positions; in Hollerith/character mode each column encodes one of 80 characters (80 bytes / 640 bits per card). The card stores program or data and performs no computation itself -- a separate card-reader peripheral feeds it to a computer.

historical_notes.claim: An 80-column card holds 80 characters; 80 columns x 12 rows = 960 bit positions.
The IBM 80-column rectangular-hole format was introduced in 1928.

usable_assumptions.confidence: n/a -- passive storage medium, performs no computation
